title: Rhaphidophora decursiva
taxonomy: Rhaphidophora decursiva is classified within the family Arum, which is part of the larger order Alismatales. This classification places Rhaphidophora decursiva among the monocotyledonous plants, characterized by having one cotyledon in their seeds. The family Arum is a group of flowering plants often referred to as the "arum family" due to its diverse range of species. Rhaphidophora decursiva, specifically, is an epiphytic species native to tropical regions. The classification of Rhaphidophora decursiva reflects its evolutionary relationships with other Arum family members and its position within the plant kingdom.
